channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Seven Nigeria Police AIGs Clear Promotion Screening, To Be Elevated To DIGs
item.description: Seven Assistant Inspectors-General of Police (AIGs) for Nigeria don pass their promotion screening to become Deputy Inspectors-General of Police (DIGs). Disu, the Inspector-General of Police, go perso...

item.title: Seven countries to skip Milan-Cortina Paralympics opening ceremony in protest of Russian participation
item.description: Organizers of the 2026 Milan-Cortina Paralympics have confirmed that seven countries will boycott the opening ceremony in protest of Russian athletes competing under their national flags. This marks t...

item.title: Escalation of US-Israel-Iran Conflict: Missile Strikes and Civilian Alerts in the Gulf
item.description: The conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran has escalated as the seventh day of hostilities unfolds across the Middle East. Iranian missile strikes targeted two hotels in Bahrain, causing fires but ...

item.title: Muturi Urges Kenya Airways to Offer Free Evacuation Flights for Kenyans in Middle East
item.description: Nairobi – DP leader Justin Muturi has strongly criticised Kenya Airways for charging citizens who are fleeing the conflict in the Middle East. He described paid flights for evacuees as “immoral and de...

item.title: Victoria Essien Explains Her Shift to Entrepreneurship and Building a Multi-Faceted Brand
item.description: Victoria Essien, wey be popular beautician and digital personality for Lagos, don open up say she dey intentionally build her brand pass just beauty mata. For one interview wey she do, she talk am cle...

item.title: Australia’s Involvement in Middle East Tensions Requires Transparency
item.description: The editorial discusses Australia’s involvement in the ongoing conflict with Iran and the need for transparency from Prime Minister Anthony Albanese. It highlights that Australian Defence Force person...

item.title: Government Strengthens Agriculture Reforms to Boost Youth Employment and Food Security
item.description: The Tanzanian government is serious about transforming the agriculture sector to reduce poverty and create jobs, especially for youth and women. Deputy Minister for Agriculture, Mr David Silinde, said...

item.title: CRDB Bank Chair Advocates for Women Leadership and Financial Literacy
item.description: Prof Neema Mori, CRDB Bank Board Chairperson, has highlighted that women can lead from any position, stressing that leadership is not only about holding formal titles. She shared these thoughts at the...

item.title: Qatar Minister Says Gulf Energy Exports Could Face Disruptions Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Qatar’s Energy Minister, Saad al-Kaabi, has warned that ongoing hostilities involving Iran could lead to a temporary halt in energy exports from Gulf countries within weeks. Speaking to the Financial ...

item.title: ODM Calls Special Delegates Convention as Leadership Dispute Over Sifuna Persists
item.description: The Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) has officially called for a Special Delegates Convention in Nairobi on March 27, 2026. The meeting, announced by acting deputy secretary general Catherine Omanyo, ...

item.title: Explosion on Bannu–Miranshah road in North Waziristan leaves one dead and 19 injured
item.description: Miranshah witnessed a tragic incident on Friday as an explosion near the Chashma Sarbandki checkpost on the Bannu–Miranshah main road in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's North Waziristan claimed the life of at le...

item.title: Asian Stock Markets Plunge Amid Rising Middle East Tensions
item.description: Asian stock markets have experienced significant drops as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East escalate. The conflict involving Iran has triggered sharp declines across major Asian indices, with S...

item.title: Royal Navy destroyer delayed, French frigate already positioned off Cyprus amid heightened tensions
item.description: RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus faced a heightened security threat following drone attacks, likely carried out by Iranian proxy Hezbollah, targeting military installations. The Royal Navy's destroyer, intended...

item.title: Australia confirms RAN personnel on US submarine amid secrecy over communications support
item.description: The Australian Defence Department has declined to confirm whether the Harold E Holt naval communication station in Western Australia supported a US submarine strike on the Iranian warship IRIS Dena. P...

item.title: MultiChoice to Close Showmax: Concerns Over Future of Local Content
item.description: MultiChoice has announced that it will soon shut down its Showmax streaming service, a move that has caught subscribers by surprise and raised concerns about the future of South African content produc...

item.title: House of Lords Committee Urges UK Government to Adopt Licensing-First Regime for AI Training on Copyrighted Material
item.description: A committee from the UK's House of Lords, the upper house of Parliament, has released a comprehensive 180-page report recommending that the government reject any policy allowing artificial intelligenc...

item.title: CISF Assigned to Ensure Security of All Indian Sea Ports
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Friday announced that the Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) will now be responsible for overseeing the security of sea ports throughout India. This was revealed...

item.title: Trump Prioritizes Iran Military Operation Over Rising Gas Prices
item.description: President Donald Trump told Reuters on Thursday that he is not worried about the rising gas prices in the U.S. which are driven by the widening conflict with Iran. He said the military operation is mo...

item.title: KUTRRH Releases Deceased Patient's Body After Intervention Over Unpaid Bill
item.description: In Nairobi, Kenyatta University Teaching, Referral & Research Hospital (KUTRRH) has finally released the body of a patient who passed away in November 2025, after it had been withheld due to an unpaid...

item.title: Heatherwick Studio Proposes Curved Residential Towers for Seoul’s Daegyo Apartments
item.description: Heatherwick Studio has unveiled an innovative plan to redevelop the Daegyo Apartments in Yeouido, Seoul. The project aims to replace the aging 1975-era complex with four new residential towers featuri...
